name: Amaretti Cookies # 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Break the almond paste into small pieces and combine it with the granulated sugar in the bowl of an electric mixer. Mix on low speed until very fine.
3. Add egg whites in 4 additions, mixing about 1 minute between each. Beat the mixture until very smooth, about 2-to-3 minutes.
4. Fill a pastry bag fitted with a ½-inch plain tube with the mixture.
5. Line two cookie sheets or jelly roll pans with parchment paper.
6. Pipe the mixture on the paper in 1-to-1½-inch mounds, about 1-inch apart.
7. Fold a clean kitchen towel into a long strip and moisten it lightly with water. Squeeze out excess water, leaving the towel more than damp.
8. Gently slap the surface of the Amaretti with the damp towel to moisten them and make the surface smooth.
9. Sprinkle the Amaretti with granulated sugar.
10. Bake for about 15-to-20 minutes, until well risen, deep golden in color, and their surface is covered with tiny cracks. Remove from the oven and place pans on racks to cool.
11. To release the cookies from the paper, turn the papers over with the cookies still adhering to them and wet the papers with hot water using a brush. Leave for a few minutes, then pull on the papers to separate the Amaretti.
Variations:
1. Amaretti di Pinoli: After moistening the piped Amaretti, strew them heavily with pine nuts, pressing lightly with fingertips so they adhere. Do not dust with sugar. Bake as for standard Amaretti.
2. Fior di Mandorla: For this variation popular in Southern Italy and Sicily, sometimes color the mixture green or pink during mixing. The rough, craggy appearance contrasts nicely with the smooth standard Amaretti. Prepare up to moistening the cookies, then dust heavily with granulated sugar and allow them to dry uncovered at room temperature for at least 12 hours. Indent each mound gently with thumb and fingers before baking as for standard Amaretti.
